title: prd-quality
description: The verifier for a PRD cell — the product defined FROM THE OUTSIDE-IN. A PRD is a spec-layer artifact (SKILL-format: intent surface + brief + an embedded ```json contract + optional references/) whose acceptance criteria are USER-FACING (what the user/market can do + experience), not technical. It is upstream of the inside-out SPEC: the SPEC realizes the PRD. The gate dimensions are mechanical (a PRD is still a well-formed, contract-bearing spec artifact, run by bin/spec-quality-check.py); the review dimensions are the outside-in judgment a calibrated critic applies. This rubric must itself be `validated` before any PRD binds to it (the rubric-maturity precondition).
maturity_precondition: This rubric cell must be validated (deterministic on fixed input; calibrated) before it gates any PRD.

dimensions.label: [gate]
dimensions.check: A well-formed spec-format artifact; cell id excludes maturity; cross-references resolve.
dimensions.fail_meaning: Not instantiable as a typed cell.

dimensions.label: [gate]
dimensions.check: Every acceptance criterion is USER-FACING and checkable — phrased as an observable thing a user/market can do or experience (not a technical assertion), and either executable or bound to a calibrated review rubric. Zero prose-only criteria.
dimensions.fail_meaning: Unobservable, inside-out, or prose-only acceptance is a hope, not a product bar.

dimensions.label: [gate]
dimensions.check: Explicit product non-goals are declared (what the product deliberately does NOT do for the user).
dimensions.fail_meaning: Unbounded product scope; the PRD admits experience it was never meant to authorize.

dimensions.label: [review]
dimensions.check: Names the target user(s) and their jobs-to-be-done — WHO this is for and WHAT they are trying to accomplish, in their terms. The product earns its requirements from a real user need, not an invented feature.
dimensions.fail_meaning: A PRD with no user + job is a feature pile with no outside; the SPEC realizes nothing in particular.

dimensions.label: [review]
dimensions.check: Defines the EXPERIENCE the user needs: the key interactions, states, feedback, and quality bar (responsiveness, clarity, error recovery) — enough that 'a good version' is recognizable from the outside, without dictating the architecture.
dimensions.fail_meaning: Without UX requirements the inside-out SPEC invents the experience; the product ships someone's guess.

dimensions.label: [review]
dimensions.check: Doneness is defined from the USER's perspective — 'a user can deal, drag a legal card, see the score change, win, and see it recorded' — a usage narrative, not a module checklist. The SPEC's technical acceptance must ENTAIL this outside-in acceptance.
dimensions.fail_meaning: Inside-out acceptance at the product layer collapses the PRD into the SPEC and loses the user.

dimensions.label: [review]
dimensions.check: The requirements cohere into ONE product with a clear value, not a disconnected pile of features. The non-goals + the jobs + the UX tell a single story.
dimensions.fail_meaning: An incoherent PRD tunnels the build toward a Frankenstein product that does many things and serves no one.

scoring.gates: ALL gate dimensions must pass; any gate failure returns the PRD to regenerating. bin/spec-quality-check.py runs the gates mechanically (a PRD is a contract-bearing spec artifact) and exits 0 iff all pass.
scoring.review: Weighted review score must clear each dimension threshold AND an aggregate of 80/100, judged by a calibrated critic that is NOT the authoring agent.
scoring.signal: On pass the CRITIC writes signals/{prd-cell}/{ts}--prd-quality.json via the validation path; signals/ is deny-on-write to the worker.
